skill.name: remote-system-maintenance
skill.description: Guides diagnostics and routine maintenance for remote Ubuntu and Debian Linux systems through structured SSH sessions.
skill.summary: Plan and document remote Linux diagnostics, cleanup, and package maintenance.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found shell command examples and weak cryptography patterns. The commands are legitimate for remote Linux maintenance, but they can change packages, remove caches, and prune logs. The weak cryptography detections at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:51 are false positives because no crypto code is present.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Remote Maintenance Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run apt update, apt upgrade, apt autoremove, apt clean, journalctl log vacuuming, snap removal, and temporary review. These commands match the purpose, but they can alter system state, remove packages, reclaim caches, and delete logs without approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed shell commands are explicit and semantically tied to remote system maintenance. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands are documented and aligned with the skill purpose.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at the description and upgrade instruction lines. No evidence found of cryptographic code, weak algorithms, encryption, hashing, or credential handling in those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding is a weak signal because the cited lines contain natural language and maintenance text, not cryptographic implementation. I did not find evidence that the skill uses or recommends weak crypto.

dangerous_patterns.title: External System Commands in Maintenance Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains direct shell commands for package upgrades, dependency removal, cache purging, log pruning, and snap revision removal. These commands should be run only on authorized hosts after reviewing expected impact.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in the skill text and can affect system state. They are not hidden, obfuscated, or unrelated to the stated maintenance purpose.

content.value_statement: Remote Linux maintenance can miss important diagnostics and leave weak audit notes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for safe checks, cleanup, and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Organizes remote Linux work into diagnostics, log review, and package assessment phases.
Lists Ubuntu and Debian cleanup steps for apt cache, orphaned packages, journals, and snap revisions.
Defines maintenance documentation fields for host identity, resource states, actions, and follow-up items.
Provides expected disk recovery ranges for common cleanup categories.
Supports SSH and tmux based maintenance planning through checklist-style guidance.
limitations: Does not execute commands or verify results by itself.
Focuses on Ubuntu and Debian systems, not all Linux distributions.
Requires operator judgment before package upgrades, removals, or log pruning.
Does not include backup, rollback, or incident response procedures.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Server Cleanup Session
use_cases.description: Create a clear checklist before reclaiming disk space on an Ubuntu or Debian server.

use_cases.title: Document Maintenance Evidence
use_cases.description: Record initial state, final state, commands run, and quantified improvements after routine maintenance.

use_cases.title: Review Remote Host Health
use_cases.description: Walk through resource usage, log health, service status, and package maintenance opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Disk Cleanup Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a safe disk cleanup session for a remote Ubuntu server. Include diagnostics, cleanup steps, and what to record.

prompt_templates.title: Remote Diagnostics Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a remote Linux diagnostics checklist for a Debian host. Cover identity, disk, memory, CPU, processes, logs, and package status.

prompt_templates.title: Maintenance Session Log
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn my maintenance notes into a structured session log with initial state, actions taken, space recovered, and follow-up recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Production Cleanup Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this planned Ubuntu production cleanup. Identify risky commands, approval checks, rollback notes, and safer sequencing before execution.

output_examples.input: Plan cleanup for an Ubuntu server with low disk space.
output: Start by recording host identity, OS version, kernel, disk usage, memory usage, and load.
Review journal size, service status, package upgrades, orphaned packages, apt cache, and disabled snap revisions.
Run approved cleanup steps in order, then document disk space recovered and remaining issues.
The session report lists the host, operator, initial resource usage, final resource usage, and each action taken.
It separates package changes, cache cleanup, journal pruning, and snap revision removal.
It ends with quantified space recovery and follow-up recommendations.

output_examples.input: Check whether my cleanup plan is safe for production.
output: The review flags package upgrades and dependency removal as change-controlled actions.
It recommends verifying backups, maintenance windows, affected services, and rollback options before execution.

best_practices: Capture baseline resource usage and host identity before running cleanup commands.
Use a tmux or similar persistent session for remote maintenance over SSH.
Document every command, result, and quantified improvement during the session.
anti_patterns: Running package upgrades on production hosts without approval or a maintenance window.
Deleting temporary files or logs before checking whether active services need them.
Reporting cleanup success without final measurements and follow-up notes.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance and checklists. The operator remains responsible for command execution and approval.

faq.question: Which systems does it target?
faq.answer: It is written for remote Ubuntu and Debian Linux systems.

faq.question: Can it help recover disk space?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers apt cache cleanup, journal pruning, disabled snap revisions, and temporary directory review.

faq.question: Is it safe for production servers?
faq.answer: It can support production maintenance, but package changes and removals require change control, backups, and operator review.

faq.question: Does it include security log review?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes authentication and security event review as part of system log checks.

faq.question: What output should users expect?
faq.answer: Users should expect checklists, ordered cleanup plans, session logs, and follow-up recommendations.
